ontology.foodon_gap_note: FOODON_03301236 represents coffee beverage as a category; no exact code for hot brewed black coffee served at a Japanese restaurant chain. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers brewed coffee generically; no specific code for hot black coffee as served at Sukiya. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34731007 refers to coffee as a substance; used as closest parent for hot brewed coffee beverage.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup of hot brewed black coffee, approximately 240 ml (240 g), as typically served at a Japanese restaurant such as Sukiya.

nutrient_source.title: Coffee, brewed from grounds, prepared with tap water (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB No. 14209)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for brewed black coffee prepared with tap water is the closest approved primary source for plain hot brewed coffee. Values represent unsweetened, black coffee with no milk or cream added, consistent with the Sukiya menu item description.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of beverages: black coffee contains no glycemic carbohydrates; GI conventionally assigned as negligible (effectively zero). Reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Black brewed coffee contains no glycemic carbohydrates (carbohydrate_g = 0.0 per 100g per USDA SR Legacy). The University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature confirm that plain black coffee has a negligible glycemic effect. GI is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value per Veritas convention) to represent effectively zero glycemic impact. GL per serving = 0.0 as carbohydrate content is zero.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (2 mg per 100 g); caffeine may cause transient blood pressure elevation
hypertension.guidance: Plain black coffee is very low in sodium and does not contribute meaningfully to dietary sodium load; however, individuals with hypertension should be aware that caffeine can cause a transient, short-lived rise in blood pressure and moderate daily intake (up to 3–4 cups) is generally considered acceptable for most hypertensive patients.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero glycemic carbohydrates; no impact on postprandial blood glucose when consumed without sugar or milk
type2_diabetes.guidance: Unsweetened black coffee contains no glycemic carbohydrates and does not raise postprandial blood glucose, making it a suitable beverage choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, provided it is consumed without added sugar, sweetened syrups, or high-fat dairy.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat and cholesterol; moderate habitual coffee consumption associated with neutral to beneficial cardiovascular outcomes in epidemiological literature
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Plain black coffee contains negligible saturated fat and cholesterol; epidemiological evidence suggests that moderate habitual consumption (3–5 cups per day) is not associated with increased cardiovascular risk and may be associated with modest protective effects, though individuals with arrhythmias should consult their clinician regarding caffeine intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Hot Coffee as served at Sukiya (a Japanese beef-bowl restaurant chain) in Singapore is a plain, unsweetened brewed black coffee served hot. It is prepared by brewing ground coffee beans with hot water, yielding a beverage that is essentially calorie-free, containing approximately 1 kcal per 100 g, with negligible protein (0.12 g), negligible fat (0.02 g), and zero glycemic carbohydrates. The beverage is naturally free of sugar, dietary fibre, and cholesterol. It contains a small amount of potassium (49 mg per 100 g) and riboflavin (0.18 mg per 100 g), consistent with USDA SR Legacy data for brewed coffee. The flavour profile is characteristically bitter with roasted, earthy notes; depth and nuance vary with the coffee bean origin and roast level. As a Fair Trade-labelled product at Sukiya, the beans are sourced under ethical trading standards. Hot Coffee is suitable for vegetarians, vegans, halal, kosher, and gluten-free diets. It has a glycemic index effectively of zero (set to 1 per Veritas convention) and a glycemic load of 0.0 per serving, making it one of the most diabetes-friendly beverage choices available. Clinically, it is low concern for hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease when consumed without added sugar or dairy. Moderate habitual coffee consumption is supported by a large body of epidemiological literature as neutral to beneficial for cardiometabolic health.
common_user_queries: Is hot coffee suitable for diabetics?
How many calories in a cup of black coffee?
Can I drink coffee if I have high blood pressure?
Is Sukiya coffee black or with milk?
What is the sodium content of black coffee?
What is the glycemic load of coffee?
meal_context: café-style beverage at Japanese fast-casual restaurant

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA SR Legacy (NDB No. 14209), which is an approved primary source for plain brewed black coffee. The food is a well-characterised, simple beverage with minimal preparation complexity. GI is supported by peer-reviewed literature and the University of Sydney GI Database convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages. Japanese local name is standard and well-attested.
notes: 1. energy_kcal is 1.0 kcal per 100 g: this is the USDA SR Legacy reported value for brewed black coffee; it is not zero but is effectively negligible. 2. carbohydrate_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for plain brewed black coffee; naturally zero as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates. 3. sugar_g is 0.0: naturally zero as no sugar is added and coffee beans contribute no free sugars to the brewed beverage. 4. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for brewed coffee. 5. saturated_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. 6. trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. 7. polyunsaturated_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy (reported as 0.001 g, rounded to 0.0). 8. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy; naturally zero as this is a plant-derived beverage. 9. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. 10. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. 11. vitamin_b6_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. 12. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0: naturally zero as this is a plant-derived beverage. 13. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0: naturally zero as coffee contains no vitamin D. 14. vitamin_e_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. 15. selenium_mcg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. 16. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate_g is 0.0. 17. protein_g is 0.12: trace amount present in brewed coffee per USDA SR Legacy; not zero. 18. fat_g is 0.02: trace amount present in brewed coffee per USDA SR Legacy; not zero. 19. The cuisine tag 'Japanese' reflects the restaurant context (Sukiya); hot brewed coffee is a globally generic beverage. 20. Cuisine tags 'Beef Dish', 'Japanese Curry', 'Grilled Chicken Skewers' from the input are restaurant-level tags for Sukiya and are not applicable to this beverage item; they have been excluded from this record. 21. monounsaturated_fat_g is 0.01 per USDA SR Legacy. 22. All -1 values in per_100g have been replaced with actual USDA SR Legacy values where available; no -1 sentinel values remain in this record.

Added Japanese local name 'ホットコーヒー' and romanized form 'Hotto Kōhī' to satisfy G6 multilingual requirement for Japanese cuisine.
Set glycemic_index to 1 (not null or 0) per zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule; gi_category set to 'low'; glycemic_load_per_serving set to 0.0.
Documented all zero macro values with justification phrases in notes per zero-macro justification rule.
Sourced all nutrient values from USDA SR Legacy NDB No. 14209 (approved source).
